center:
i
<truncated 5069 bytes>
ything else supports at different update rhythms.
center.2-anti-patterns: No flashing numbers. No live badges on every element. One live dot in header. Currency of data IS the signal.
center.3-references: MLB Gameday diamond. Bloomberg real-time feed. ESPN scoreboard compact.
center.4-typography: Score at 2.5rem is hero. Count at 1.125rem is second — the immediate situation. Pitch count at 1rem. Play descriptions at .75rem body text.
center.5-spacing: Scoreboard: generous (20px padding). Play feed: tight (7px rows) with scroll. Sidebar: compressed. Three density levels serving different attention modes.
center.6-color: Bases: diamond-on/diamond-off (text-1/rule in dark). No green/red for movement — arrows in text-3. Team colors only on logo bars. Scoring plays get text-1 presence.
center.7-mobile: Scoreboard stays full-width hero. Diamond shrinks proportionally. Play feed is primary. Odds compress to 2-column prices. Bullpen moves to expandable.
center.8-footer: No footer on live pages. Every pixel is content.
center.9-squint: Score (2.5rem) and inning label visible. Diamond shape visible. Play feed visible as a text block. Nothing else registers.
center.10-removal: No win probability graph. No spray chart. No pitch velocity tracker. No advanced metrics. Score, situation, plays, odds, pitcher status, bullpen. That's the live page.
